perturbation.original_text: Either Party may disclose Confidential Information to the other Party in confidence provided that the disclosing Party identifies such information as proprietary and confidential either by marking it, in the case of written materials, or, in the case of information that is disclosed orally or written materials that are not marked, by notifying the other Party of the proprietary a n d confidential nature of the information, such notification to be done orally, by e-mail or written correspondence, or via other means of communication as might be appropriate.
perturbation.changed_text: Either Party may disclose Sensitive Information to the other Party in confidence provided that the disclosing Party identifies such information as proprietary and confidential either by marking it, in the case of written materials, or, in the case of information that is disclosed orally or written materials that are not marked, by notifying the other Party of the proprietary a n d confidential nature of the information, such notification to be done orally, by e-mail or written correspondence, or via other means of communication as might be appropriate.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Sensitive Information' in Section 1 creates a contradiction. The rest of the agreement consistently refers to 'Confidential Information,' including the definitions in the introduction. Thus, Section 1 now speaks of disclosing a different type of information ('Sensitive Information') without clearly defining it or establishing its relationship to 'Confidential Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: When informed of the proprietary and confidential nature of Confidential Information that has been disclosed by the other Party, the receiving Party (“Recipient”) shall, for a period of three (3) years from the date of disclosure, refrain from disclosing such Confidential Information to any contractor or other third party without prior, written approval from the disclosing Party and shall protect such Confidential Information from inadvertent disclosure to a third party using the same care and diligence that the Recipient uses to protect its own proprietary and confidential information, but in no case less than reasonable care. The Recipient shall ensure that each of its employees, officers, directors, or agents who has access to Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement is informed of its proprietary and confidential nature and is required to abide by the terms of this Agreement. The Recipient of Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall promptly notify the disclosing Party of any disclosure of such Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement or of any subpoena or other legal process requiring production or disclosure of said Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Sensitive Information' in the Changed Text creates a contradiction with the Contradicted Text. The Contradicted Text explicitly refers to 'Confidential Information' and outlines the responsibilities related to its handling. By introducing 'Sensitive Information' without defining it or establishing its relationship to 'Confidential Information', the Changed Text creates ambiguity and makes it unclear whether the obligations in the Contradicted Text apply to 'Sensitive Information' as well. This misaligned terminology directly conflicts with the Contradicted Text, fitting the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: When informed of the proprietary and confidential nature of Confidential Information that has been disclosed by the other Party, the receiving Party (“Recipient”) shall, for a period of three (3) years from the date of disclosure, refrain from disclosing such Confidential Information to any contractor or other third party without prior, written approval from the disclosing Party and shall protect such Confidential Information from inadvertent disclosure to a third party using the same care and diligence that the Recipient uses to protect its own proprietary and confidential information, but in no case less than reasonable care.
perturbation.changed_text: When informed of the proprietary and confidential nature of Confidential Information that has been disclosed by the other Party, the receiving Party (“Recipient”) shall, for a period of three (3) years from the date of disclosure, refrain from disclosing such Proprietary Data to any contractor or other third party without prior, written approval from the disclosing Party and shall protect such Confidential Information from inadvertent disclosure to a third party using the same care and diligence that the Recipient uses to protect its own proprietary and confidential information, but in no case less than reasonable care.
perturbation.explanation: In section 2, replacing 'Confidential Information' with 'Proprietary Data' in the phrase 'refrain from disclosing such Proprietary Data' introduces an inconsistency. The rest of the section, and indeed the agreement, consistently refers to 'Confidential Information'. The recipient is now obligated to refrain from disclosing something not previously defined or consistently used, which leads to confusion about which type of data is protected by this specific clause. The obligation to protect 'Confidential Information' still exists, but the specific prohibition now refers to something else.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party and nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights to such Confidential Information on the other Party. The Recipient shall honor any request from the disclosing Party to promptly return or destroy all copies of Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement and all notes related to such Confidential Information. The Parties agree that the disclosing Party will suffer irreparable injury if its Non-Disclosure Agreement -Page 2 of 3- CONFIDENTIAL Confidential Information is made public, released to a third party, or otherwise disclosed in breach of this Agreement and that the disclosing Party shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against a threatened breach or continuation of any such breach and, in the event of such breach, an award of actual and exemplary damages from any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Proprietary Data' in a key obligation: refraining from disclosure. The contradicted text consistently refers to 'Confidential Information' as the subject of the agreement's protections and obligations. By switching the term in the changed text, the receiving party is now told to refrain from disclosing 'Proprietary Data' in one instance, while the rest of the contract, including the contradicted text, focuses on 'Confidential Information'. This creates a direct conflict about what information is protected by this specific non-disclosure clause, leading to uncertainty and a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party and nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights to such Confidential Information on the other Party. The Recipient shall honor any request from the disclosing Party to promptly return or destroy all copies of Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement and all notes related to such Confidential Information. The Parties agree that the disclosing Party will suffer irreparable injury if its Non-Disclosure Agreement -Page 2 of 3- CONFIDENTIAL Confidential Information is made public, released to a third party, or otherwise disclosed in breach of this Agreement and that the disclosing Party shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against a threatened breach or continuation of any such breach and, in the event of such breach, an award of actual and exemplary damages from any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Material disclosed under this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party and nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights to such Confidential Information on the other Party. The Recipient shall honor any request from the disclosing Party to promptly return or destroy all copies of Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement and all notes related to such Confidential Information. The Parties agree that the disclosing Party will suffer irreparable injury if its Non-Disclosure Agreement -Page 2 of 3- CONFIDENTIAL Confidential Information is made public, released to a third party, or otherwise disclosed in breach of this Agreement and that the disclosing Party shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against a threatened breach or continuation of any such breach and, in the event of such breach, an award of actual and exemplary damages from any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Material' at the start of Section 3 creates a contradiction. The remainder of Section 3, and indeed the rest of the document, refers exclusively to 'Confidential Information.' The question is whether 'Confidential Material' has a different, potentially broader or narrower, scope than what has been defined as 'Confidential Information.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Material disclosed under this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party and nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights to such Confidential Information on the other Party. The Recipient shall honor any request from the disclosing Party to promptly return or destroy all copies of Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement and all notes related to such Confidential Information. The Parties agree that the disclosing Party will suffer irreparable injury if its Non-Disclosure Agreement -Page 2 of 3- CONFIDENTIAL Confidential Information is made public, released to a third party, or otherwise disclosed in breach of this Agreement and that the disclosing Party shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against a threatened breach or continuation of any such breach and, in the event of such breach, an award of actual and exemplary damages from any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Material' at the beginning of the paragraph introduces a contradiction. The rest of the paragraph, and the document, consistently uses 'Confidential Information.' By using 'Confidential Material' in the beginning, it raises ambiguity as to whether 'Confidential Material' is interchangeable with 'Confidential Information', or if it has a different scope or meaning. Since the contract doesn't define them, this creates an ambiguity and potential in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The disclosing party acknowledges that the Recipient may currently or in the future be developing information internally, or receiving information from other parties, that is similar to the Confidential Information. Nothing in this Agreement will prohibit the Recipient from developing or having developed for it products, concepts, systems or techniques that are similar to or compete with the products, concepts, systems or techniques contemplated by or embodied in the Confidential Information provided that the Recipient does not violate any of its obligations under this Agreement in connection with such development.
perturbation.changed_text: The disclosing party acknowledges that the Recipient may currently or in the future be developing information internally, or receiving information from other parties, that is similar to the Protected Information. Nothing in this Agreement will prohibit the Recipient from developing or having developed for it products, concepts, systems or techniques that are similar to or compete with the products, concepts, systems or techniques contemplated by or embodied in the Confidential Information provided that the Recipient does not violate any of its obligations under this Agreement in connection with such development.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Information' only in the first instance in Section 4 creates ambiguity. The rest of the paragraph still refers to 'Confidential Information.' It is unclear whether 'Protected Information' is intended to be a synonym for 'Confidential Information' or if it refers to different information, and what the implications are for the recipient's obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The disclosing party acknowledges that the Recipient may currently or in the future be developing information internally, or receiving information from other parties, that is similar to the Protected Information. Nothing in this Agreement will prohibit the Recipient from developing or having developed for it products, concepts, systems or techniques that are similar to or compete with the products, concepts, systems or techniques contemplated by or embodied in the Confidential Information provided that the Recipient does not violate any of its obligations under this Agreement in connection with such development.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Information' in the first instance, while the rest of the paragraph and the contradicted text still refers to 'Confidential Information,' creates a strong ambiguity. This ambiguity directly contradicts the intention of clarity and consistency within the agreement, as it becomes unclear whether 'Protected Information' and 'Confidential Information' are interchangeable or refer to distinct types of information. This falls under the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' as the contract now uses multiple terms without defining them, potentially leading to conflicting obligations and enforcement challenges.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the above, the Parties agree that information shall not be deemed Confidential Information and the Recipient shall have no obligation to hold in confidence such information, where such information: (a) Is already known to the Recipient, having been disclosed to the Recipient by a third party without such third party having an obligation of confidentiality to the disclosing Party; or (b) Is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of the Recipient, its employees, officers, directors, or agents; or (c) Is independently developed by the Recipient without reference to any Confidential Information disclosed hereunder; or (d) Is approved for release (and only to the extent so approved) by the disclosing Party; or (e) Is disclosed pursuant to the lawful requirement of a court or governmental agency or where required by operation of law.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the above, the Parties agree that material shall not be deemed Confidential Information and the Recipient shall have no obligation to hold in confidence such information, where such information: (a) Is already known to the Recipient, having been disclosed to the Recipient by a third party without such third party having an obligation of confidentiality to the disclosing Party; or (b) Is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of the Recipient, its employees, officers, directors, or agents; or (c) Is independently developed by the Recipient without reference to any Confidential Information disclosed hereunder; or (d) Is approved for release (and only to the extent so approved) by the disclosing Party; or (e) Is disclosed pursuant to the lawful requirement of a court or governmental agency or where required by operation of law.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'information' with 'material' in the introductory sentence of Section 5 introduces potential confusion. The rest of the section then uses 'information' to describe what is *not* considered 'Confidential Information'. This inconsistency creates uncertainty whether the exceptions apply only to specific forms of 'material' (e.g., documents) or to all 'information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding the above, the Parties agree that material shall not be deemed Confidential Information and the Recipient shall have no obligation to hold in confidence such information, where such information: (a) Is already known to the Recipient, having been disclosed to the Recipient by a third party without such third party having an obligation of confidentiality to the disclosing Party; or (b) Is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of the Recipient, its employees, officers, directors, or agents; or (c) Is independently developed by the Recipient without reference to any Confidential Information disclosed hereunder; or (d) Is approved for release (and only to the extent so approved) by the disclosing Party; or (e) Is disclosed pursuant to the lawful requirement of a court or governmental agency or where required by operation of law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'information' with 'material' in the clause defining what is *not* considered Confidential Information. The rest of the section, including the contradicted text which is identical to the changed text, continues to use 'information'. This creates a contradiction because the initial clause now refers to 'material' while the exceptions (a) through (e) refer to 'information'. It's unclear whether the exceptions apply to 'material' or only to 'information', leading to uncertainty and potential disputes about which information is protected, which adheres to the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.
